MEDB18AudiometryNASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for audiometric assessment. Captures pre- and postflight hearing threshold data to monitor crew hearing status.Questionnairehttps://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/Questionnaire/medb-1-8-audiometry#0.5.8
overviewMedical Requirements Overview0..1group
overview.mrid-numberMRID Number and Title0..1displayInitial Value: string = MEDB 1.8 Audiometry
overview.sponsorSponsor1..1choiceOptions: 1 option
Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Sponsor Organizations#medical-operations "Medical Operations"
overview.disciplineDiscipline1..1choiceOptions: 1 option
Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Medical Disciplines#audiology "Audiology"
overview.purposePurpose/Objectives1..1textInitial Value: string = To assess pre- and postflight hearing thresholds on crew members using pure tone audiometry. To track hearing changes across missions.
overview.measurement-parametersMeasurement Parameters1..1stringInitial Value: string = Pure tone air conduction thresholds at 500, 1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, 6000, 8000 Hz
overview.flight-durationFlight Duration1..1stringInitial Value: string = >= 30 days

preflight-activities.descriptionActivity Description0..1textInitial Value: string = Pure tone audiometry is performed in a sound-treated booth. Testing includes air conduction thresholds at standard frequencies.

preflight-activities.scheduleSchedule1..1stringInitial Value: string = Annual medical examination and L-60 to L-10 days

Documentation for this format

Options Sets

Answer options for overview.sponsor

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-mrid-sponsor-cs#medical-operations ("Medical Operations")

Answer options for overview.discipline

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-mrid-discipline-cs#audiology ("Audiology")

Answer options for preflight-activities.hardware

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-medical-hardware-cs#audiometer ("Audiometer")

Answer options for preflight-activities.location

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-medical-facility-cs#jsc ("Johnson Space Center")

Answer options for preflight-audiometry-results.interpretation

  • http://snomed.info/sct#162339002 ("Hearing normal")
  • http://snomed.info/sct#15188001 ("Hearing loss")
  • http://snomed.info/sct#95820000 ("Bilateral hearing loss")

Answer options for postflight-audiometry-results.interpretation

  • http://snomed.info/sct#162339002 ("Hearing normal")
  • http://snomed.info/sct#15188001 ("Hearing loss")
  • http://snomed.info/sct#95820000 ("Bilateral hearing loss")

Answer options for postflight-audiometry-results.comparison

  • No significant change
  • Threshold shift detected
  • Unable to compare

Answer options for data-delivery.archive

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-data-archive-cs#mmis ("Medical Mission Information System")

extension.valueString: The astronaut/crewmember subject of this medical requirement

name: MEDB18Audiometry
title: MEDB 1.8 Audiometry
description: NASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for audiometric assessment. Captures pre- and postflight hearing threshold data to monitor crew hearing status.
purpose: To assess pre- and postflight hearing thresholds on crew members using pure tone audiometry
copyright: NASA Johnson Space Center - Medical Requirements Integration Documents (MRIDs) JSC28913

initial.valueString: To assess pre- and postflight hearing thresholds on crew members using pure tone audiometry. To track hearing changes across missions.
Pure tone air conduction thresholds at 500, 1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, 6000, 8000 Hz
Pure tone audiometry is performed in a sound-treated booth. Testing includes air conduction thresholds at standard frequencies.
Annual medical examination and L-60 to L-10 days

item.text: N/A - Included as part of Medical Operations overall debrief
